name: Kids Help Phone
address: 1200 Ave McGill College, Suite 1680, Montréal, QC H3B 4G7 (Head Office)
hours_text: Phone: 24/7, 365 days/year. Text: 24/7. Live Chat: Daily 7:00 PM to Midnight ET.
description: Canada’s only 24/7, national support service offering professional counselling, information, and referrals for young people. Services include crisis support via phone or text, professional counselling, and a peer-to-peer support community.

name: Trans Lifeline
address: 548 Market Street, PMB 15337, San Francisco, CA 94104 (Head Office)
hours_text: Hotline: Monday to Friday, 2:00 PM – 10:00 PM ET.
application_process: Call the toll-free number. No referral required.
description: A grassroots hotline and non-profit organization offering direct emotional and financial support to trans people in crisis. Run by and for trans people, offering peer support without non-consensual active rescue (no calls to 911 without consent).

name: Hope for Wellness Helpline
hours_text: Phone & Chat: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
application_process: Call 1-855-242-3310 or visit website for chat.
description: Offers immediate mental health counselling and crisis intervention to all Indigenous people across Canada. Services are available via telephone and online chat, provided by experienced and culturally competent counsellors.

name: Assaulted Women's Helpline (AWHL)
address: P.O. Box 40569, Etobicoke, ON M9B 6K8 (Mailing only)
hours_text: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
application_process: Call or text. No referral required.
description: Provides anonymous and confidential crisis counselling, safety planning, emotional support, information, and referrals to women experiencing any form of abuse. Services are available in over 200 languages.

name: Trellis HIV & Community Care
hours_text: Office: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 4:45 PM (Closed 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM). Needle Exchange: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM & 1:00 PM – 4:30 PM.
description: Provides support for people living with or at risk of HIV/AIDS and STBBIs. Services include harm reduction (needle exchange), naloxone distribution, supportive counselling, and education.

name: Trellis HIV & Community Care
hours_text: Office: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 4:45 PM (Closed 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM). Needle Exchange: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM & 1:00 PM – 4:30 PM.
description: Provides support for people living with or at risk of HIV/AIDS and STBBIs. Services include harm reduction (needle exchange), naloxone distribution, supportive counselling, and education.

name: One Roof Kingston Youth Hub
hours_text: Drop-in: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM (Some programming until 7 PM).
description: A one-stop community hub providing wrap-around services for vulnerable and homeless youth, including mental health support, housing help, and life skills.

name: Victim Services of Kingston and Frontenac
address: 705 Division St, Kingston, ON K7K 4C2 (Inside Police HQ)
hours_text: Office: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Crisis: 24/7 via Emergency Services referral.
application_process: Call or referral by police/emergency services.
documents_required: Police report or incident number often required for VQRP+ funding.
description: Provides immediate crisis intervention, practical assistance, and support to victims of crime and tragic circumstances. Administers VQRP+ for financial assistance.

name: Hotel Dieu Hospital Urgent Care Centre (KHSC)
hours_text: Daily: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM (Registration may close early if capacity is reached).
description: Provides assessment and treatment for non-life-threatening injuries and illnesses (e.g., cuts, sprains, infections) that cannot wait for a family doctor.

name: Kingston General Hospital (KGH) Emergency Department
address: 41 King Street West, Kingston, ON K7L 2V7
hours_text: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
description: A full-service Level 1 Trauma Centre providing 24/7 care for life-threatening injuries and severe illnesses.

name: KFL&A Public Health Dental Services
hours_text: Monday - Friday: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM (By appointment only).
documents_required: Health Card, proof of income (for seniors/Healthy Smiles application).
description: Provides dental screening, cleaning, and fluoride for eligible children and seniors. Administers Healthy Smiles Ontario and OSDCP.

name: Kingston Community Legal Clinic
hours_text: Mon-Thu: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Fri: 1:00 PM – 4:30 PM.
fees: Free legal services (clients may pay disbursements).
description: Provides free legal advice and representation to low-income residents regarding housing, income support, and employment law.

name: Salvation Army Housing Resource Centre
hours_text: Mon, Tue, Thu, Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Wed: 1:00 PM – 4:30 PM.
documents_required: ID, proof of income/expenses for financial assistance.
description: Provides housing loss prevention, housing search assistance, and emergency homelessness diversion. Not an emergency shelter.

name: Transgender Health Program (KCHC)
address: 263 Weller Ave, Unit 4, Kingston, ON K7K 2V4
hours_text: Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM. Wed: 8:00 AM – 7:30 PM.
application_process: Self-referral accepted. Call to book intake.
description: Provides gender-affirming health care including hormone therapy initiation, surgical planning/referrals, and social transition support.

name: Street Health Centre (KCHC)
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Closed 12-1 PM). Sat-Sun: 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM.
documents_required: None required for harm reduction; Health card preferred for medical.
fees: Medical services covered by OHIP (care provided regardless of card status).
description: Provides low-barrier health services for marginalized populations, including needle exchange, opioid substitution therapy, Hepatitis C treatment, and safer consumption services.

name: Kingston Interval House
address: P.O. Box 21042, Kingston, ON K7L 5P5 (Mailing Only)
hours_text: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
description: Emergency shelter and support services for women, children, and youth experiencing domestic violence.

name: Telephone Aid Line Kingston (TALK)
hours_text: Daily: 6:00 PM – 2:00 AM.
description: A confidential, anonymous, non-judgmental listening service for anyone who needs to talk. Run by volunteers to provide support and information.

name: Elizabeth Fry Society of Kingston
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM.
description: Provides housing support and community residential facilities for women, particularly those in conflict with the law or at risk.

name: St. Vincent de Paul Society of Kingston
hours_text: Pantry/WearHouse: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM. Lunch: Mon-Fri 11:30 AM – 1:00 PM.
application_process: Drop-in for lunch/clothing. Register for pantry.
description: Offers emergency food pantry, free clothing (WearHouse), and a daily lunch program (Loretta Hospitality).

name: Lunch by George (Outreach St. George's Kingston)
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 9:00 AM – 12:30 PM (Breakfast 9-10:30, Lunch 11-12:30).
description: Provides complimentary hot breakfast and lunch to those in need. Also offers clothing and social support.

name: Salvation Army Rideau Heights Corps
hours_text: Food Bank: Tue & Thu 11:00 AM – 12:00 PM (By Appointment).
description: Provides emergency food bank services and community meals to the Rideau Heights neighborhood.

name: Partners in Mission Food Bank
hours_text: Mon-Thu: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:00 PM. (Closed 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM daily).
application_process: Appointment required. Call to book. No walk-ins.
description: Provides emergency food hampers (approx. 7 days of food) to Kingston residents.

name: Martha's Table
hours_text: Mon-Fri: Breakfast 9-11 AM; Lunch 12-2 PM; Dinner 3-5 PM. Sat-Sun: Lunch 12-2 PM (Takeout).
description: Provides nutritious meals in a safe and welcoming environment. Offers indoor dining and takeout options.

name: The Good Food Box Kingston
address: 263 Weller Ave, Kingston, ON K7K 2V2 (Main KCHC Site)
hours_text: Pickup: 3rd Thursday of month. Office: Mon-Fri 8:30-4:30.
application_process: Order online or at host sites by 2nd Thursday of month.
description: Non-profit fresh fruit and vegetable distribution program. Participants order boxes at low cost and pick up on the third Thursday of the month.

name: AMHS-KFLA 24/7 Crisis Line
address: 552 Princess Street, Kingston, ON K7L 1C7 (Walk-in Office)
hours_text: Crisis Line: 24/
application_process: Call the crisis line or walk in during office hours.
description: Provides immediate response, assessment, and stabilization for individuals experiencing a mental health or addiction crisis. Mobile crisis team available.

name: Kingston Detoxification Centre (KHSC)
hours_text: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
documents_required: Health card preferred but not required.
description: A short-term, non-medical residential service for individuals withdrawing from alcohol or drugs. Provides 24-hour support and monitoring.

name: Kingston Youth Shelter
address: 365 Nelson St, Kingston, ON K7K 4M9 (Emergency Shelter)
hours_text: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
application_process: Call or drop-in. First come, first served.
description: Provides emergency shelter, food, and hygiene supplies for homeless youth. Also operates transition homes.

name: Sexual Assault Centre Kingston (SACK)
address: 400 Elliott Avenue, Unit 1, Kingston, ON K7K 6M9
hours_text: Crisis Phone: 24/
description: Provides free, confidential support for survivors of sexual violence. Services include 24/7 crisis line, counselling, and accompaniment to medical/legal appointments.

name: Maltby Centre
address: 31 Hyperion Court, Suite 100, Kingston, ON K7K 7G3
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM.
description: Provides mental health and autism services for children and youth.

name: Resolve Counselling Services Canada
hours_text: Mon, Wed: 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM. Tue, Thu: 8:30 AM – 8:00 PM. Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:00 PM.
description: Offers individual, family, and financial counselling.

name: Kingston Home Base Non-Profit Housing
hours_text: Office: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Shelter: 24/7 (Intake usually evening).
description: Operates "In From The Cold" emergency shelter (adults/families) and supportive housing programs.

name: Ryandale Transitional Housing
hours_text: Mon-Sun: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM (Staffed hours).
description: Provides sober living transitional housing for men experiencing homelessness. Focus on life skills and stabilizing housing.

name: Dawn House
description: Transitional and supportive housing for marginalized or vulnerably housed women. Empowering women to find permanent solutions.

name: Kingston Community Health Centres (KCHC) - Weller Clinic
address: 263 Weller Ave, Unit 4, Kingston, ON K7K 2V4
hours_text: Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM. Wed: 8:00 AM – 7:30 PM. (Phones answered 9-12, 1-4).
description: A primary health care clinic providing medical care, nutrition, and social work support to residents of North Kingston.

name: KFL&A Public Health: Sexual Health Clinic
hours_text: Quick Test Clinic (Walk-in): Tue & Thu 1:00 PM – 3:30 PM. Appointments: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM.
application_process: Walk-in for Quick Test; Call for other appointments.
documents_required: Health card not required for Quick Test.
fees: Most services free; some contraceptives at cost.
description: Provides confidential sexual health services including STI testing, birth control, and emergency contraception.

name: CDK Family Medicine and Walk-In Clinic
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM. Sat-Sun: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM.
fees: Covered by OHIP. Uninsured fees apply.
description: A walk-in medical clinic treating minor illnesses and injuries for patients without a family doctor.

name: Queen's Legal Aid
address: 303 Bagot Street, Suite 500, Kingston, ON K7K 5W7
hours_text: Mon-Thu: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Fri: 1:00 PM – 4:30 PM.
description: Provides legal services to low-income residents and students, handling summary conviction offences, tenant disputes, and tribunal hearings.

name: City of Kingston - Housing and Social Services (Ontario Works)
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM.
documents_required: Proof of income, assets, shelter costs, ID.
description: Provides financial and employment assistance to people in financial need.

name: Kingston Frontenac Public Library - Central Branch
hours_text: Mon-Thu: 9 AM – 8 PM. Fri-Sat: 9 AM – 5 PM. Sun: 1 PM – 5 PM.
documents_required: ID for card.ConclusionThis audit confirms that the KFL&A community service sector is robust but complex. The primary risks for data accuracy—and consequently for user access—lie in the nuances of operating hours (e.g., chat vs. phone) and the shifting physical locations of housing services. Regular quarterly verification of these high-volatility data points is recommended to maintain the integrity of Kingston Care Connect. By adopting the precise address corrections and operational distinctions detailed in this report, the directory will significantly enhance its reliability as a navigation tool for Kingston's most vulnerable residents.
description: Main public library branch offering books, internet access, and community programs.

name: KEYS Job Centre
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. (Tue open to 6:00 PM).
description: Employment and newcomer services offering job search assistance, workshops, and settlement support.

name: Seniors Association Kingston Region
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM.
description: A community centre for older adults offering recreation, education, and social programs.

name: Community Living Kingston and District
address: 541 Days Rd, Unit 6, Kingston, ON K7M 3R8
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM.
description: Provides residential and community support services for individuals with intellectual disabilities.

name: ReStart Employment Services
hours_text: Mon-Thu: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:00 PM.
description: Offers employment services and housing support to help individuals find work and maintain housing.

name: ACFOMI (Association canadienne-française de l'Ontario)
hours_text: Mon: 8:30 AM – 6:00 PM. Tue-Thu: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Fri: 8:30 AM – 3:00 PM.
description: Provides employment and settlement services for French-speaking newcomers and residents.

name: YMCA of Eastern Ontario - Kingston
hours_text: Mon-Thu: 5:30 AM – 9:00 PM. Fri: 5:30 AM – 7:00 PM. Sat-Sun: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM.
description: Provides fitness, recreation, and childcare services.

name: BGC South East
hours_text: Programs: Mon-Fri 3:00 PM – 8:00/8:30 PM.
description: Provides after-school programs, camps, and recreation for children and youth.

name: H'art Centre
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 8:30 AM – 3:30 PM.
description: An arts hub providing programming for adults with developmental disabilities and those facing barriers.

name: Loving Spoonful
address: 263 Weller Ave, Kingston, ON K7K 2V4 (KCHC Site)
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM.
description: Connects people with healthy food through fresh food reclamation, community gardens, and cooking workshops.

name: Ongwanada
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM.
description: Supports people with developmental disabilities to lead full lives in their communities.

name: Kingston Military Family Resource Centre (KMFRC)
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM.
description: Provides support services, childcare, and mental health resources for military families.
